mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent while core inflation remains below 3.0 percent indicates a healthy demand-supply balance that expands corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% AND CPIAUCSL annual change < 3.0%
failure_condition: Forward returns in the triggered regime do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger corporate revenue visibility, which directly supports equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts exceeding 100,000 units signals robust construction activity and household formation, which historically precedes broad-based economic expansion and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing start spikes fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which drives sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative median forward return over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt outstanding reflects sustained credit availability and household leverage, which historically correlates with positive equity market performance over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS changes show no statistically significant predictive power for 5-day forward SPX returns (t-stat < 1.0) across a 20-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the annualized CPI inflation rate, real interest rates turn positive, increasing the discount rate on future cash flows and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of periods where real rates are positive, or the real rate spread shows no monotonic relationship with equity returns.
